title: insert_spendable_note helper not atomic: crash leaves orphan note
description: wallet/src/db.rs insert_spendable_note (test helper, #[cfg(test)], line ~1482) calls INSERT INTO notes then self.upsert_witness() as two separate operations without a transaction. Trigger: process crash between the two calls. Bad behavior: note row exists with no witnesses row; spendable_notes() INNER JOINs witnesses so the note never appears as spendable; insert_note_with_witness() (production path) wraps both in a transaction and avoids this, but the test helper does not — meaning tests can pass while exercising a non-representative code path. Also, if the test inserts a note successfully but upsert_witness fails for any reason, the note_id is returned as if successful but the note is permanently unspendable.
